titleStmt.title: Letter from Philip C. MacDermot, 27 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Philip C. MacDermot

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter was written by Philip MacDermot (b. 1863), the crown solicitor for County Roscommon.
This letter refers to a case of cattle driving off of the Mather’s Lands in Ballinasloe, County Galway. According to a telegram received by MacDermot from the district Inspector twenty-three of those who participated in the cattle drive have been arrested and the remaining participants could not be located.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ROCKMOUNT,
BOYLE. July 27, 1916 Ballinasloe Crimes Act Cases
Cattle driving off Mathers
lands Dear Sir After office hours
this evening I got a telegram
from the District Inspector
R.I.C stating that 23
of the defts. have been
arrested, the remaining
deft could not be found.
ROCKMOUNT,
BOYLE.
RE The Deft.who is
not arrested. (Harvey)
is not an important
deft, in fact he is
a former from the
town of Ballinasloe
& how he came to be
at the cattle drive
Saturday. So if
writing to me
to Haydens Hotel
Ballinasloe. If Depts. Counsel
perhaps the Attorney
General may consider
engaging Counsel for the
Crown but I am not
asking for this unless
you & The Attorney General
consider it advisable. Yours faithfully Philip C. MacDermot
